# Project Brief

<!-- Generated by /bootstrap from the questionnaire. Do not edit manually unless updating the project scope. -->

## What This Is

A local desktop portal for Yedidya website admins. It wraps multiple WordPress automation actions (member list generation, user deletion, document upload, etc.) in a simple GUI — fields, dropdowns, buttons. No server, no web hosting — each admin runs it locally on their own PC.

## Goals

- **Primary:** A working portal with credentials management (Windows Credential Manager) and the members-list action fully integrated
- **Ongoing:** Add more WordPress admin actions over time as needed

## Audience

Yedidya website admins — a small group of people with admin-level WordPress access.

## Where Things Stand

The members-list pipeline already exists as a standalone Python solution in `sub-projects/Yedidya-members-list`. That logic needs to be wrapped inside the portal as one of its actions.

## Planned Actions

1. **Members list** — generate a new version and upload to WordPress (existing sub-project)
2. **Delete users** — accept a list, delete from WordPress
3. **Upload document** — accept a Word file, convert to PDF, upload to a selectable WordPress location
4. *(More to be added over time)*

## Technical Constraints

- Runs locally on Windows — no server required
- Credentials stored in Windows Credential Manager (not plain files)
- Must be distributable to other admins — they each run it on their own machine
- Simple UI only — no visual design required
- No over-engineering: simplest thing that works

## Done Looks Like

An admin opens the portal, their credentials are either already stored or they enter them once. They pick an action, fill in any required fields, press a button, and it runs.

## Out of Scope (for now)

- Server-side deployment
- Non-admin users
- Automated scheduling / event triggers
